heading: Assessment procedures
label: 4908 Assessment procedures
notes: Amendments1993—Subsec. (a). , designated existing provisions as par. (1) and added par. (2).Pub. L. 103–189, § 8(g)(1)Subsec. (b). , inserted “and importers” after “Handlers”.Pub. L. 103–189, § 8(g)(2)Subsec. (c)(1). , inserted “or importers” after “handlers”.Pub. L. 103–189, § 8(g)(3)
amendments: Amendments1993—Subsec. (a). , designated existing provisions as par. (1) and added par. (2).Pub. L. 103–189, § 8(g)(1)Subsec. (b). , inserted “and importers” after “Handlers”.Pub. L. 103–189, § 8(g)(2)Subsec. (c)(1). , inserted “or importers” after “handlers”.Pub. L. 103–189, § 8(g)(3)

children.heading: Persons responsible for remittance of assessments; recordkeeping; equal and unitary assessments
children.label: a Persons responsible for remittance of assessments; recordkeeping; equal and unitary assessments

children.text: Each handler required to pay assessments under a plan, as provided for under , shall be responsible for payment to the Board, as it may direct, of the assessments. A handler also shall collect from any producer, or shall deduct from the proceeds paid to any producer, on whose watermelons a producer assessment is made, the assessments required to be paid by the producer. The handler shall remit producer assessments to the Board as the Board directs. Such handler shall maintain a separate record with respect to each producer for whom watermelons were handled. Such records shall indicate the total quantity of watermelons handled by the handler, including those handled for producers and for the handler, the total quantity of watermelons handled by the handler that are included under the terms of the plan, as well as those that are exempt under the plan, and such other information as may be prescribed by the Board. To facilitate the collection and payment of assessments, the Board may designate different handlers or classes of handlers to recognize differences in marketing practices or procedures used in any State or area. The handler shall be assessed an equal amount as the producer. No more than one assessment on a producer nor more than one assessment on a handler shall be made on any watermelons.section 4906(f) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: If importers are subject to the plan, each importer required to pay assessments under the plan shall be responsible for payment of the assessment to the Board, as the Board may direct.

children.label: B
children.text: The assessment on imported watermelons shall be equal to the combined rate for domestic producers and handlers and shall be paid by the importer to the Board at the time of the entry of the watermelons into the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: the total quantity of watermelons imported into the United States that are included under the terms of the plan;

children.label: ii
children.text: the total quantity of watermelons that are exempt from the plan; and

children.label: iii
children.text: such other information as may be prescribed by the Board.

children.label: D
children.text: No more than 1 assessment shall be made on any imported watermelon.

children.heading: Inspection of records
children.label: b Inspection of records
children.text: Handlers and importers responsible for payment of assessments under subsection (a) shall maintain and make available for inspection by the Secretary such books and records as required by the plan and file reports at the times, in the manner, and having the content prescribed by the plan, to the end that information and data shall be made available to the Board and to the Secretary that is appropriate or necessary to the effectuation, administration, or enforcement of this chapter or of any plan or regulation issued under this chapter.

children.heading: Confidentiality of information; disclosure authority; general or violation statements; penalties; removal from office
children.label: c Confidentiality of information; disclosure authority; general or violation statements; penalties; removal from office

children.text: the issuance of general statements based on the reports of a number of handlers or importers subject to a plan if such statements do not identify the information furnished by any person; or
the publication by direction of the Secretary of the name of any person violating any plan together with a statement of the particular provisions of the plan violated by such person.
